signal: Hub advisors must own their local state in WAI-Hub/advisors/{name}/ following the historian pattern (scan_state.json, passes.jsonl, vectors.jsonl, reports/). Advisors lean on shared infrastructure (hub-registry.json, WAI-State.json) for awareness but never write advisor-specific fields to shared files. This keeps the registry clean and makes each advisor self-contained and discoverable. Pattern established with gardener advisor, applies to librarian and future advisors.
rationale: Without this principle, each new advisor adds fields to hub-registry.json, creating coupling and clutter. The historian pattern (spoke-level) already proved this works. Extending it to hub-level advisors creates a consistent, scalable architecture.
